local_dishes.name: Madra
local_dishes.description: The dish mainly consists of the soaked chickpeas (chana) or vegetables
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dhaam
local_dishes.description: The dish includes dal, rajma, rice, curd, boor ki Kadi and is very well complemented with gur (jaggery)
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tudkiya Bhath
local_dishes.description: The authentic pulao of Himachal Pradesh
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Bhey
local_dishes.description: A delicious dish which is prepared with the lotus stems. Thinly sliced lotus stems are then cooked in the ginger-garlic, onions and gram flour
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sidu
local_dishes.description: Made from wheat flour, Sidu is a local side-dish of Himachal Pradesh which greatly complements the main course
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Babru
local_dishes.description: A flat bread which is prepared with the stuffing of black gram paste which is added to the kneaded dough
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Aktori
local_dishes.description: A festive dish which is very much enjoyed by the people of Himachal Pradesh during their festive time. Aktori is prepared in the form of a cake or pancake made with the buckwheat leaves which is further cooked in the wheat flour
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Mittha
local_dishes.description: The sweet dish of Himachal Pradesh prepared on special occasions. It has sweetened rice mixed with dry fruits and raisin
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Kaale Channe ka Khatta
local_dishes.description: A traditional Pahari Dish which is sour in taste and is usually served with rice. This dish is usually served with Madra
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Aloo Palda
local_dishes.description: Soft boiled potatoes are cooked in a thick gravy of either yoghurt or buttermilk
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Auriya Kaddu
local_dishes.description: Also known as kaddu ka khatta is a simple pumpkin curry
source_files: North India/All Inclusive Guide- Kufri, Shimla, Chandigarh.pdf

local_dishes.name: Himachali Siddu
local_dishes.description: Traditional steamed wheat bun stuffed with poppy seeds, walnuts, or lentils, typically served with ghee or dal
source_files: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx

local_dishes.name: Himachali Dham
local_dishes.description: Traditional festive meal including rice with madra (chickpea curry), rajma, dal, and curd-based gravies, cooked with aromatic spices
source_files: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx

safety_tips.tip: Avoid walking in isolated areas late at night, especially in unfamiliar surroundings
safety_tips._source_file: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx

safety_tips.tip: Keep cash, phones, and valuables secure, particularly in busy markets
safety_tips._source_file: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx

safety_tips.tip: Carry a charged mobile phone and emergency contacts when exploring
safety_tips._source_file: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx

safety_tips.tip: Follow local guidelines when visiting mountain viewpoints, waterfalls, or trekking trails
safety_tips._source_file: North India/Mr. Amit Sinkar- North India- All Inclusive Guide.docx
